toolsUsed: docker compose runtime rebuild with explicit Flyway-v2 + MailHog overrides
docker exec psql password reset and reversible lifecycle-state probes

assertions.title: Tenant admins cannot mutate global settings while approved runtime read surfaces stay available with published date-time semantics

steps.action: Fetch global settings as super-admin, replay the same payload as a tenant admin, and then fetch the surviving tenant-runtime and portal read surfaces as the tenant admin
steps.expected: Tenant admin global-settings mutation is denied, while tenant-runtime metrics and portal read surfaces remain available and keep string date-time fields aligned with the published contract
steps.observed: On the compose-backed runtime, `GET /api/v1/admin/settings` returned 200 for `uat.superadmin@example.com`, but replaying that payload through `PUT /api/v1/admin/settings` as `ut.taab.admin2@example.com` returned 403 with `data.code=AUTH_004`. The same tenant-admin session successfully fetched `GET /api/v1/admin/tenant-runtime/metrics` plus `/api/v1/portal/dashboard`, `/operations`, and `/workforce`, and each response exposed wrapper `timestamp` as a string while metrics `data.policyUpdatedAt` also came back as a string.

steps.action: Run the Lane 01 regression and contract subsets that cover admin settings, lifecycle, runtime enforcement, portal payloads, and OpenAPI parity
steps.expected: The claim stays green in targeted automated coverage
steps.observed: The core Lane 01 Maven subset finished with `BUILD SUCCESS; Tests run: 147, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0`, and the portal/OpenAPI subset finished with `BUILD SUCCESS; Tests run: 6, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0`.

tests.coreLane01: BUILD SUCCESS; Tests run: 147, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0
tests.portalOpenApi: BUILD SUCCESS; Tests run: 6, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0

assertions.title: Lifecycle vocabulary persists compatible stored values and unknown stored values fail closed

steps.action: Drive the live super-admin lifecycle controls on tenant `UTTAAB2` and inspect the stored `companies.lifecycle_state` values
steps.expected: Supported API vocabulary remains `SUSPENDED`/`ACTIVE`/`DEACTIVATED` while stored values remain schema-compatible (`HOLD`/`ACTIVE`/`BLOCKED`)
steps.observed: `POST /api/v1/superadmin/tenants/2/suspend` returned 200 with `data.status=SUSPENDED` while the stored value became `HOLD`. A follow-up `POST /api/v1/superadmin/tenants/2/activate` returned 200 with `data.status=ACTIVE` and restored the stored value to `ACTIVE`. Separately, `POST /api/v1/superadmin/tenants/2/deactivate` returned 200 with `data.status=DEACTIVATED` while the stored value became `BLOCKED`.

steps.action: Probe protected endpoints during suspended, deactivated, and intentionally corrupted stored-lifecycle states
steps.expected: Suspended tenants keep the documented protected-read path, deactivated tenants lose protected access, and unknown stored values fail closed instead of reopening access
steps.observed: While tenant `UTTAAB2` was suspended, the already-issued tenant-admin bearer token still reached `GET /api/v1/auth/me` with status 200. After deactivation, the same protected read returned 403 with `reason=TENANT_LIFECYCLE_RESTRICTED`. After temporarily dropping `chk_companies_lifecycle_state` and setting `companies.lifecycle_state` to `UNSUPPORTED_TEST_STATE`, both `GET /api/v1/auth/me` and `GET /api/v1/admin/users` returned 403; the admin-user response carried `reason=TENANT_LIFECYCLE_RESTRICTED` and `reasonDetail=Tenant is deactivated` before the row and constraint were restored to ACTIVE.

steps.action: Run the lifecycle and runtime-enforcement regression pack
steps.expected: Converter, controller, and runtime enforcement coverage stays green
steps.observed: The Lane 01 core subset passed with 147 tests, including `CompanyLifecycleStateConverterTest`, `TenantLifecycleServiceTest`, `CompanyControllerIT`, `SuperAdminControllerIT`, `TenantRuntimeEnforcementServiceTest`, `TS_RuntimeTenantRuntimeEnforcementTest`, and `TS_RuntimeCompanyContextFilterExecutableCoverageTest`.

evidence.tests: BUILD SUCCESS; Tests run: 147, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0

assertions.title: One authoritative runtime-policy write and enforcement path remains

steps.action: Warm a tenant-admin bearer session, update runtime policy through the canonical company-scoped path, and immediately replay the warmed bearer token
steps.expected: The same runtime-policy model that the canonical write path updates is enforced immediately on the handling node
steps.observed: `GET /api/v1/auth/me` for `ut.taab.admin2@example.com` returned 200 before the change. `PUT /api/v1/companies/2/tenant-runtime/policy` as `uat.superadmin@example.com` then returned 200 with `data.state=BLOCKED` and `data.reasonCode=INCIDENT-LOCKDOWN`. Replaying the preexisting tenant-admin bearer token immediately after the write returned 403 with `reason=TENANT_BLOCKED`, and restoring the same canonical path to ACTIVE returned 200; the next `/auth/me` call recovered to 200.

The Lane 01 core subset passed with 147 tests, including `CompanyControllerIT`, `TenantRuntimeEnforcementServiceTest`, `TS_RuntimeTenantPolicyControlExecutableCoverageTest`, and `TS_RuntimeTenantRuntimeEnforcementTest`.

evidence.tests: BUILD SUCCESS; Tests run: 147, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0

assertions.title: TEN-09 remains closed by a completed validation-first bundle with current runtime/OpenAPI parity evidence

steps.action: Review the sealed TEN-09 validation-first bundle and its attached runtime/OpenAPI artifacts
steps.expected: The bundle records exact commands, payload evidence, verdict, and reviewer sign-off for the questioned tenant-runtime and portal surfaces
steps.observed: The sealed packet `docs/code-review/executable-specs/01-lane-control-plane-runtime/00-ten-09-validation-first-bundle.md` cites the exact targeted Maven commands, the compose-backed runtime rebuild and password reset commands, saved route captures under `artifacts/ten-09-*.json`, the verdict `confirmed backend defect`, and the packet-governance reviewer sign-off that blocked wider lane work until parity was fixed.

steps.action: Re-probe the live tenant-runtime metrics and portal routes and re-run the portal/OpenAPI regression subset
steps.expected: Current runtime behavior matches the surviving published contract, confirming the Lane 01 timestamp-parity fix closed the earlier TEN-09 mismatch
steps.observed: In this validation session, `GET /api/v1/admin/tenant-runtime/metrics` plus `/api/v1/portal/dashboard`, `/operations`, and `/workforce` all returned 200 on the compose-backed runtime for the seeded UTTAAB2 tenant-admin session. The runtime wrapper `timestamp` fields and metrics `policyUpdatedAt` are now strings instead of numeric epochs, and the `PortalInsightsControllerIT` + `OpenApiSnapshotIT` subset passed 6/6 tests.

evidence.tests: BUILD SUCCESS; Tests run: 6, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0

frictions.description: Launching the delegated `user-testing-flow-validator` subagent failed immediately with `Invalid model: custom:CLIProxyAPI-5.4-high`.
frictions.resolution: Fell back to direct in-session validation, consistent with mission guidance and the existing helper-launch limitation note in `.factory/library/user-testing.md`.

frictions.description: The compose-backed actuator endpoint still reported `503 {"status":"DOWN"}` while the target API routes on `8081` were usable.
frictions.resolution: Used the target API routes themselves as the primary runtime evidence and recorded the degraded actuator note instead of treating it as a blocker.

summary: Validated all four Lane 01 API/runtime assertions directly in-session after the delegated flow-validator launch failed. Tenant-admin global-settings mutation remained denied while the surviving runtime metrics and portal read surfaces were available with string date-time fields, lifecycle vocabulary persisted compatible stored values and failed closed for unsupported stored states, the canonical company-scoped runtime-policy path enforced immediately on the warmed bearer session, and the sealed TEN-09 validation-first bundle remained backed by fresh runtime/OpenAPI parity evidence.
